subject: GRB 200710A: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely SHORT GRB

At 00:52:07 UT on 10 Jul 2020, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 200710A (trigger 616035132.072806 / 200710036).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 77.9, Dec = 4.0 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 05h 11m, 4d 00'), with a statistical uncertainty of 16.6 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 62.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200710036/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn200710036.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200710036/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn200710036.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2020/bn200710036/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn200710036.gif
